This plant is further classified under the subclass Rosidae and the division Magnoliophyta. The family Molliniaceae is a small family of flowering plants that is endemic to China. intro: Mollia polycarpoides, also known as the Chilean pepper tree, is a plant species in the family Molliniaceae, native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Mollia polycarpoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mollia polycarpoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, coughs, and sore throats.
